channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: US court tells Israeli company to stop targeting WhatsApp
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 18 (Reuters) - A U.S. court has ordered Israel's NSO Group to stop targeting Meta Platforms WhatsApp messaging service, a development the spyware company warned could put it out of business. In a 25-page ruling handed down Friday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Raphael Satter, NewsNation

item.title: Music could help ease pain from surgery or illness. Scientists are listening
item.description: Hospitals and doctors' offices are inviting singers and musicians to help patients manage their pain, as music's ability to reduce pain is gaining attention.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What Sora’s Martin Luther King Jr. problem revealed to OpenAI
item.description: Martin Luther King Jr. did plenty to change the world for the better. And nearly 60 years after his assassination, he’s at the center of a major concession by the world’s leading AI company that puts a battle over intellectual property and the right to control one’s image into the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Claude just got a skill upgrade and it’s coming for your workflows
item.description: Model builders are moving beyond simple AI chatbots to creating comprehensive assistants that, in the words of AI dignitary Ethan Mollick, “do real work” in enterprise workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Threat actors are spreading malicious extensions via VS marketplaces
item.description: Careless developers publishing Visual Studio extensions to two open marketplaces have been including access tokens and other secrets that can be exploited by threat actors, a security vendor has found.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: White House joins Bluesky and immediately trolls Trump opponents
item.description: The White House has joined Bluesky, the social media platform of choice of many in the left-leaning online world.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hi-fi legend Quad's new super-powerful, super-connected stereo amp could be all the amp you need for the rest of your life
item.description: Money No Object: Quad launches its "finest-ever" integrated amp, and it's future-proofed in a ton of ways
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: A new DFARS rule could block defense contracts for firms with foreign consulting ties
item.description: "Even though those carve outs might limit application to specific activities the firms do, it may not completely cover all the activities," said Diz Locaria. The post A new DFARS rule could block defense contracts for firms with foreign consulting ties first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: I see dead people on Sora, and I’m conflicted about it
item.description: Hello again from Fast Company and thanks for reading Plugged In. Before I go any further, a bit of quick self-serving promotion: This week, we published our fifth annual Next Big Things in Tech list.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: It's called automated officiating. The NBA is utilizing it to get even more calls right
item.description: The play, in real time to the naked eye, might have looked very close to a violation. LeBron James leaped, got his right hand on the ball with a few tenths of the game’s final second remaining and tapped it through the basket to give the Los Angeles Lakers a buzzer-beating win last season.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Don't fall for fake settlement sites that steal your data
item.description: Red flags like processing fees, urgent countdowns and requests for full Social Security numbers expose fraudulent settlement sites targeting consumers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Federal, industry IT leaders on how agencies are preparing for post-quantum cybersecurity
item.description: Air Force, CISA, NIST share how they are preparing for a future when quantum computers could break today’s encryption. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Federal, industry IT leaders on how agencies are preparing for post-quantum cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: How to run RAG projects for better data analytics results
item.description: The arrival of generative AI-enhanced business intelligence (GenBI) for enterprise data analytics has opened up access to insights, while also increasing the speed, relevance and accuracy of those insights. But that’s in best-case scenarios.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5
